result:
Added local note versioning with `history` and `restore`, wired through the existing mutation paths in [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go). Notes now save snapshots under `notes/.history/<note-id>/` for create, CLI edit, editor-based edit, web edit, archive/unarchive, rename link rewrites, delete, and restore. `history <id>` lists saved versions, `history <id> <version>` prints a line diff against the current note, and `restore <id> <version>` rolls a note back or recreates a deleted note.

I also updated [main_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main_test.go) with coverage for history listing, diff output, rollback, deleted-note recovery, editor-triggered version capture, and the new command routing, and documented the new behavior in [README.md](/home/changkun/null-codex/README.md).

I could not run `gofmt` or `go test` in this sandbox because `gofmt` and `go` are not installed here.
